_comment: Recall floors for `osaurus-evals run --fail-on-floor`. Keys are case `id` strings — must match the fixture id exactly. `minMatches` is the per-lane recall floor (the gate sums matched names across the tools/methods/skills lanes, so method/skill cases are guarded, not just tools); a case's `maxAccepted` (read from its fixture) is also enforced for any floored case that declares one. The flag is OPT-IN and not yet wired into CI. PROMOTED 2026-06-19 (W3 retrieval): (1) `capability_search.abstain-greeting` and `capability_search.method-abstain` now enforce `maxAccepted=0` via the query-intent abstain gate (`CapabilityQueryIntent.isConversationalAbstain`, applied in BOTH `CapabilitySearch.search` and `CapabilitySearchEvaluator.evaluate`) — a pure chit-chat query ('thanks, that's perfect') short-circuits before BM25/embed/RRF so no saturation-noise hit is accepted. This is the pre-RRF gate the 2026-06-19 sweep (Config/capability-search-sweep.md) called for; it does NOT raise any cosine floor, so `browser-prefix` recall is unaffected (verified: 8/5). (2) `capability_search.skill-paraphrase` now enforces `minMatches=1` — `Content Summarizer`'s keyword array was enriched with paraphrase synonyms ('gist', 'overview', 'recap', 'synopsis', 'brief', 'abstract', 'main-points', 'takeaways', 'highlights', 'skim') so the embedder bridges 'give me the gist of this article' (verified: skills matched 1/1). Earlier promotion 2026-06-19: `capability_search.skill-abstain` (`maxAccepted=0`) — the tools-lane embed-cosine gate drops its lone noise hit; the intent gate now also covers its 'good morning' query. STILL tracking-only (intentionally OMITTED): `capability_search.shell-execution` — `sandbox_exec` is excluded from the index by design (ToolIndexService.syncFromRegistry runtimeManagedToolNames: runtime-managed tools are auto-exposed when their agent flag is on and have no capabilities_load carve-out, so surfacing them in capability search would advertise a capability the discovery/load path can't action). No recall fix can rescue it while the indexer-side exclusion stays, and the exclusion is correct — kept tracking-only, not force-passed. The method/skill recall floors were calibrated by the PR-A baseline against the per-lane embed-cosine cutoffs `minimumRelevanceScoreMethods` = `…Skills` = 0.25; the tools recall floors hold under the tools-lane gate (0.25 < min recall cosine 0.298).
